steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize a difference between Bengali and Danish nouns specifically. The correct choice must directly contrast how nouns behave in each language — not focus on language families or other properties.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To indicate location in Bengali, a suffix is appended to a noun, but in Danish, the noun is unchanged and a preposition is used.’: Directly contrasts noun behavior in both languages: Bengali modifies the noun with a suffix; Danish leaves the noun unchanged and uses a preposition. This is exactly the difference between the two languages' nouns. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Bengali and Danish belong to two distinct language families; the former is in the Indo-Aryan family, while the latter is a Germanic language.’: Focuses entirely on language family classification, not noun behavior. Fails to address the difference in how nouns work. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Though nouns in Bengali change form to indicate location, this Indo-Aryan language belongs to a different language family than Danish does.’: Mentions Bengali noun change but then shifts focus to language family rather than contrasting with Danish noun behavior. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘In Bengali, nouns take a different form when they function as a location, as the language belongs to the Indo-Aryan family; Danish, however, belongs to the Germanic family.’: Begins with Bengali noun behavior but resolves the contrast by citing language families rather than Danish noun behavior. The comparison is incomplete and off-focus. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
